question:
Will Chelsea win on 2025-10-25?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for October 25, 2025
If Chelsea wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.premierleague.com/

evidences.title: Enzo Maresca delivers Chelsea team news ahead of ...
evidences.content: Maresca is aware of the challenge that awaits his Chelsea side tomorrow evening, and he confirmed at his pre-match press conference that we will still be without a handful of players. 'Cole (Palmer) is still out,' Maresca said. 'Trevoh is also suspended [after receiving a red card against Brighton], and we have seven or eight players out for tomorrow's game. 'I don't have any update at the moment [on Palmer]. We have a meeting this week to see how he is and we will then decide [whether he can return after the international break]. Maresca was able to welcome back Benoit Badiashile from injury in midweek and the French defender produced a composed performance in the heart of the Chelsea backline before being replaced in the latter stages by Josh Acheampong. [...] Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca will be unable to call upon 'seven or eight players' for Liverpool's visit to Stamford Bridge due to injuries and suspension. The Blues host Arne Slot's side in our final fixture before the international break, and we will look to build upon the hard-fought victory secured over Benfica in Champions League action on Tuesday. Liverpool, in contrast, were beaten in their midweek European assi...

evidences.title: Chelsea FC injury update: Reece James, Cole Palmer and Andrey Santos latest news and return dates | The Standard
evidences.content: Potential return date: October 18, 2025 vs Nottingham Forest (A) ## Cole Palmer Cole Palmer has been out of action since being forced off after just 20 minutes in the 2-1 loss to Manchester United last month as a troublesome groin injury flared up again. Maresca later revealed that the attacking midfielder, who also starred in the Allianz Arena three days prior, wasn’t fully fit ahead of the Man United game in a decision that backfired. Absent: Cole Palmer Getty Images The latest on Palmer is that a meeting is set to take place to determine the next steps. Speaking ahead of the Liverpool game, Maresca said: “I don't have any update at the moment [on Palmer]. We have a meeting this week.” [...] The good news, as revealed by Willy Caballero, is the 19-year-old’s substitution was just a precaution. “The two central defenders, it was just prevention,” Cabellero told reporters. “They made a great effort today, but just prevention. We spoke with them already and they said that they are okay, so [it is] good for us.” Potential return date: October 18, 2025 vs Nottingham Forest (A) ## Benoit Badiashile Benoit Badiashile made his first appearance for 94 days when he started against Benfi...

evidences.title: Key dates for Chelsea’s 2025/26 season | News | Official Site | Chelsea Football Club
evidences.content: A post shared by Chelsea FC (@chelseafc) 1-9 September – The first of the 2025/26 season’s international windows arrives early in the campaign, with most countries playing two matches during that period. Tuesday 16 September – The opening fixtures of the 2025/26 Champions League league phase will be played, as Chelsea return to the competition following last season’s fourth-place finish in the Premier League. Wednesday 24 September – Chelsea’s Carabao Cup campaign will begin, when we enter the competition in the third round along with the rest of the sides competing in Europe. Saturday 4 October – We will face the reigning champions, as Premier League winners Liverpool come to west London. 6-14 October – Club football will take another break for an international window. [...] Saturday 1 November – We travel to the London rivals Tottenham Hotspur in the Premier League. 10-18 November – The last international window of 2025 arrives, with countries looking to finalise their place at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. 20-30 December – The festive season is one of the highlights of the football calendar in England and we will have three enticing clashes over an 11-day period. evidences.title: 2025-26 Premier League top 4 odds: Will there be a surprise Champions League entrant? - The Athletic
evidences.content: Spurs are the unknown quantity this term. They qualified for the Champions League by winning the dismal Europa League final, but sensibly still decided that the league form was too poor to continue with Ange Postecoglou at the helm. He has been replaced by Thomas Frank after his years of fine work at Brentford. The fixture congestion is not ideal. Nor is the knee injury that will sideline James Maddison indefinitely. Spurs need to recruit, having seemingly missed out on the signature of target Morgan Gibbs-White. Finishing in the top four would be a remarkable achievement for Frank, especially considering Spurs ended last season one place above the relegation zone. [...] Betfair have Chelsea at 8/15 to finish in the top four, something they only just managed under Enzo Maresca last season, securing Champions League football on the final day. Optimism abounds, though, especially after an unlikely Club World Cup triumph that few would have predicted before the tournament began. Estêvão’s arrival from Palmeiras is a major boon for the Stamford Bridge faithful as the Brazilian starlet looks to impress ahead of the World Cup next summer. [...] Arsenal are at odds of 2/11, unsurprisin...

evidences.title: Chelsea 2025-26 season preview: Added firepower, Colwill loss but continuity to be celebrated - The Athletic
evidences.content: ### What are they worried about? Colwill’s long-term knee injury has removed the only consistent presence from the heart of Maresca’s defence. Chelsea’s abbreviated summer rest and pre-season may yet leave them at a significant physical disadvantage relative to their Premier League rivals, even if it does not manifest immediately. Eight games of Champions League football will also be a more exacting midweek test than last season’s six Conference League mismatches. Maresca’s squad management is going to matter more than ever. The injured Colwill will be missed (Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images) ### How’s the manager feeling? [...] Joao Pedro continued a spectacularly clinical start to his career at the tip of Maresca’s attack with a goal in each game, and Gittens showcased some sharp skills and explosive speed on the left flank. But it was Estevao who truly captured the imagination of Stamford Bridge against Leverkusen, thrilling both in combination with Cole Palmer and as a solo performer. Palmer and Estevao threaten to be an entertaining combination (Visionhaus/Getty Images) ### What are people excited about? The convincing manner of that 3-0 victory over Paris Saint-Germain in th...

Enzo Maresca's FIFA Club World Cup winners are searching for a lift after losing back-to-back Premier League matches, but they will be without "seven or eight" key players through injury and suspension for Saturday’s encounter at Stamford Bridge, according to the head coach. [...] ##### Why are Chelsea letting in so many goals? Chelsea have conceded seven times across their last three Premier League fixtures and must raise their resilience levels against opponents who have netted in each of their last 40 top-flight outings. evidences.content: Delap made an immediate favourable impression (Richard Pelham/Getty Images) Jamie Gittens was not acquired from Borussia Dortmund in time to contribute to Chelsea’s success in America, but is expected to play a significant role on the left flank this season. On the right, Brazilian prodigy Estevao has finally completed his highly anticipated transfer from Palmeiras and could provide an X factor in what will be his first season in England. Advertisement In defence, Jorrel Hato’s arrival from Ajax helps soften the blow of Levi Colwill tearing his ACL in a training session at Cobham. The 19-year-old Dutchman is more experienced than his age would suggest and is equally adept at playing left-back or centre-back.
